scores.correctness_notes: The response describes v4 of the library with detailed Python API (TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, CertificateAvailableEvent, renew_certificate, regenerate_private_key, etc.) which may exist but is completely different from what the gold standard describes. The gold standard focuses on the interface specification (v1 charmlibs), the CSR/databag contract, error codes (101, 102, 103, 201), and the requirement to stop using revoked certificates. The agent's answer doesn't mention the interface contract requirements, the error codes, or the charmlibs package at all.
scores.hallucination_notes: The specific APIs described (TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, CertificatesRequirerCharmEvents, renewal_relative_time, refresh_events, regenerate_private_key, renew_certificate) may exist in some version of the library but are not verifiable against the gold standard and appear to describe a different library version entirely. The gold standard explicitly notes that event names like certificate_available are part of the Python API not the interface spec documented in charmlibs — so the agent's detailed event descriptions may be fabricated or from an incompatible version.
